key_insight: A large autoregressive generative model can directly design heterogeneous catalysts conditioned on both categorical descriptors and binding energies, enabling controllable inverse design rather than brute-force screening.
engineering_relevance: This approach could substantially accelerate catalyst discovery by generating candidate structures that are already biased toward target reaction conditions, reducing search cost in materials workflows.
method: Pretrained a GPT-style catalyst generator on 133 million structures.
Fine-tuned on ~460,000 optimized structures with categorical labels and binding energies.
Used numerical embeddings to condition one autoregressive model on mixed discrete and continuous properties.
evidence_signal: The model reported 98% structural validity, 95% optimization validity, 93% joint match for adsorbate type and composition, and about a 1.5 to 4-fold screening efficiency gain.
watch_item: Binding-energy conditioning matched the target only about 20% of the time, so property control is improved but still not fully reliable for deployment.
paper_title: Toward Controllable Catalyst Inverse Design via Large-Scale Autoregressive Pretraining
